$schema: Hand-curated approvals for third-party packages whose licenses fall outside the auto-approved permissive set. Each entry suppresses the package from the 'Review Required' list and is rendered in the 'Approved Exceptions' section of THIRD_PARTY_LICENSE_AUDIT.md and THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.txt. Add a new entry only after reviewing the package's license terms and confirming compatibility with Nimbalyst's distribution.

approvals.name: @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk
approvals.reason: Anthropic commercial license (https://code.claude.com/docs/en/legal-and-compliance). Permits use by developers writing code locally, which is exactly how Nimbalyst integrates the SDK. Used unmodified.

approvals.name: elkjs
approvals.reason: Eclipse Public License 2.0. Bundled unmodified as a JavaScript library. EPL-2.0 is a file-level weak copyleft that permits this without affecting Nimbalyst's licensing; only modifications to elkjs itself would need to be released under EPL-2.0.

approvals.name: libheif-js
approvals.reason: GNU Lesser GPL 3.0. Bundled unmodified as a JavaScript module loaded at runtime from node_modules; users can substitute their own build. License text is included in THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.txt. No copyleft contagion to Nimbalyst's source.
